title: Employ Identifier Management
description: Employ identifier management for users and devices by receiving authorization before assigning an identifier, preventing reuse of identifiers, and disabling identifiers after a defined period of inactivity.

checks.name: Inactive User Accounts are Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify accounts that have not logged in within the defined inactivity period (e.g., 60 days) are disabled

checks.name: Guest Account is Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm the built-in Guest account is disabled on this system

checks.name: Account Naming Follows a Defined Convention
checks.purpose: Verify local accounts follow a defined naming convention that prevents ambiguity or reuse of identifiers

checks.name: Inactive User Accounts are Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify accounts that have not logged in within the defined inactivity period are disabled in AD or locally

checks.name: Guest Account is Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm the built-in Guest account is disabled on this server

checks.name: Account Naming Follows a Defined Convention
checks.purpose: Verify accounts follow a defined naming convention preventing reuse of identifiers for different users

checks.name: Terminated User Accounts are Promptly Disabled or Removed
checks.purpose: Confirm no active accounts exist for users who are no longer authorized by checking last logon against HR records or an IAM system

checks.name: Inactive User Accounts are Locked
checks.purpose: Verify the INACTIVE field in /etc/shadow or useradd defaults is set to lock accounts after the defined inactivity period

checks.name: Guest and Anonymous Accounts are Locked or Removed
checks.purpose: Confirm accounts named guest, nobody, or anonymous have no valid shell and are locked in /etc/shadow

checks.name: Unique UIDs are Not Reused
checks.purpose: Verify no UID has been reused across current and recently removed accounts by checking /etc/passwd against deleted account UIDs

checks.name: Inactive User Accounts are Locked
checks.purpose: Verify the INACTIVE field in /etc/shadow is set to lock accounts after the defined inactivity period on Debian

checks.name: Guest and Anonymous Accounts are Locked or Removed
checks.purpose: Confirm accounts named guest or nobody have no valid shell and are locked in /etc/shadow on Debian

checks.name: Unique UIDs are Not Reused
checks.purpose: Verify no UID has been reused across current and recently removed accounts on Debian

remediation: Configure account inactivity lockout policy via GPO (disable after 60 days of inactivity). Disable the Guest account. Establish and enforce an account naming convention. On Linux, set INACTIVE in /etc/default/useradd and use passwd -l to lock inactive accounts. Implement an offboarding process to promptly disable departed user accounts.
